ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

Notes: 1=At least 4 times per year (every 3 months); 2=About 2-3 times per year (every 4-6 months); 3=About once per year (every 12 months); 4=Less often than once per year; 999=Not applicable; -10=Missing

ElementDescription: Do you alternate between daily PrEP and on-demand (2-1-1 PrEP dosing) use?
If you intended to use on-demand PrEP, why have you decided to switch from once-daily oral PrEP to on-demand oral PrEP?
Notes: 1=Because I am having sex less frequently; 2=Because I want to take fewer pills; 3=Because I want to reduce the cost of my pills; 4=Because I want fewer side effects; 5=Because my partner(s) changed; 6=Other; 999=Not applicable; -10=Missing

ElementDescription: When you intended to use on-demand PrEP, how many times have you taken on-demand dosing in the past 3 months?
When you intended to use on-demand PrEP, how frequently did you remember to take the double-dose 24 hours before your sexual encounter in the past 3 months?
When you intended to use on-demand PrEP, how many hours on average before your sexual encounter did you take your double-dose in the past 3 months?
Notes: 1=0-1 hours; 2=2-5 hours; 3=6-10 hours; 4=11-15 hours; 5=16-20 hours; 6=21-24 hours; 999=Not applicable; -10=Missing

ElementDescription: When you intended to use on-demand PrEP, how frequently did you remember to take your single dose 24 hours after the double-dose in the past 3 months?
Are you prescribed daily oral PrEP or on-demand PrEP?

How confident do you feel about the effectiveness of on-demand PrEP versus once-daily PrEP?
Notes: 1=2-1-1 PrEP is less effective than once-daily PrEP; 2=2-1-1 PrEP is as effective as once-daily PrEP; 3=2-1-1 PrEP is more effective compared to once-daily PrEP; 999=Not applicable; -10=Missing

ElementDescription: What barriers have you experienced with the 2-1-1 dosing strategy?
Notes: 1=Unplanned sexual encounters (i.e., missing the double dose 2-24 hours before a sexual encounter); 2=Trouble remembering the dosing schedule after the double dose (i.e., at 24 and 48 hours after the double dose); 3=Lack of healthcare provider knowledge about 2-1-1 dosing; 4=Cost associated with prescription and lack of insurance coverage; 5=Stigma; 6=Alcohol/drug use or mental health challenges; 7=Other; 999=Not applicable; -10=Missing

ElementDescription: If you ''alternate'' between daily PrEP and on-demand PrEP, why have you decided to switch back to daily oral PrEP?
Notes: 1=Because of increasing risk; 2=Partner's request; 3=Not sure about the effect of on-demand PrEP; 4=Other; 999=Not applicable; -10=Missing
